items.title: Kalshi and Polymarket are billion dollar bets banned from Australia — for now

children.content: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.

items.title: Bondi shooting victim labelled 'subhuman' in online abuse of inquiry witnesses

children.content: Victims of antisemitism have been targeted with death threats and depicted as animals in AI-generated images after giving evidence to the royal commission examining the Bondi terror attack.

image.altText: NSW Police at royal commission hearing

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight

children.content: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of yesterday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

image.altText: A Qatar plane on the tarmac at Melbourne international airport.

images.altText: Cameron Munster and Tom Dearden hold the Origin shield

children.content: New South Wales hosts the State of Origin opener in Sydney as Queensland looks for an ambush in enemy territory. Follow live.

updates.title: What time does State of Origin start tonight?

items.title: What we know, and what we don't, about new changes to Centrelink system

children.content: The federal government has proposed changes to Australia's $2 billion employment services system to make them “more responsive, effective and dignified”.

image.altText: Centrelink sign.

items.title: Chalmers flags 'legitimate' small business CGT debate

children.content: The government is treading carefully on carve-outs to its capital gains tax legislation amid backlash to its plan.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

items.title: No 'plan B' for submarines if AUKUS fails, says defence minister

children.content: The defence minister brushes off calls for a "plan B" in case AUKUS fails, declaring it would be a step towards "giving up" on acquiring any new submarines at all.

image.altText: A submarine moves through the water causing a massive wake behind it, some crew visible above deck.

items.title: Russia looks for new soldiers in Transnistria as war casualty numbers grow

children.content: The Kremlin is trying to recruit more soldiers for its war in Ukraine but an analyst says at the rate of casualties, "Russia cannot replace recruits fast enough to keep the 'meat grinder' fed."

image.altText: Russian President Vladimir Putin  reaches his hand across a table

items.title: This 24yo started gambling as a teenager and has now lost 900k

children.content: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teenagers gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

items.title: Bystanders foil alleged kidnap attempt of woman changing tyre in Sydney

children.content: A 27-year-old woman was changing a tyre when a man allegedly threatened her with a knife and attempted to drag her into an SUV, NSW Police said.

image.altText: A man in a tracksuit is handcuffed while surrounded by several NSW Police officers.

items.title: ABC's Justin Stevens resigns as director of news

children.content: Justin Stevens, the ABC's director of news, has announced his resignation after 19 years with the organisation.

image.altText: Headshot of Justin Stevens

items.title: Record-breaking rain welcomed in drought-hit parts of NSW

children.content: Up to 100 millimetres of rain has been welcomed across drought-hit parts of western NSW, but some farmers expecting relief were left disappointed.

image.altText: Wet paddocks through the window of a ute driving through the landscape

items.title: Rodent faeces, animal blood, mould spark $60k penalty for country bakery

children.content: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread and a jar of rodent faeces at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

image.altText: A wide of a shop front

items.title: Australian travellers urged to check measles immunity

children.content: Authorities keep a close eye on measles outbreaks in Asia, as many cases reported in Australia continue to be returned overseas travellers.

image.altText: A vial of the measles, mumps, and rubella (MMR) vaccine is pictured

items.title: Cuts of meat that will give you more bang for your buck

children.content: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

image.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

children.content: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.title: Bulk carrier rescues two fishermen and Smokey the dog after boat sinks

children.content: An emergency distress beacon activation off the North Queensland coast yesterday afternoon sparked a coordinated rescue involving a bulk carrier and rescue jet.

items.title: Brother of hit-run victim says family will appeal NT court's 'light' sentence

children.content: The family of a man who was left to die alongside his severely injured brother and father after a hit-and-run crash, say they will appeal the "light" sentence handed to the man who was behind the wheel.

image.altText: A coat of arms on a building, alongside the words: Supreme Court.

items.title: NT child protection bill one year in the making had 'limited' consultation

children.content: Despite taking a year to prepare a controversial bill surrounding child protection, the NT Department of Children and Families says consultation with organisations was "limited".

image.altText: Front-on shot of white man, pale blue shirt, and bright blue striped tie, sitting at long white desk, inside high-rise office

items.title: Council buys land for $1, but what's the catch?

children.content: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

image.altText: A drone image of a block of land with bushland on surrounded by buildings, road and the beach.

items.title: Teaching student charged with online child abuse offences

children.content: A Victorian teaching student, who had recently worked as a childcare centre cook, a foster carer
and was a school council president, is facing charges relating to online child abuse material.

image.altText: A man with glasses smiles at the camera.

items.title: NASA has revealed its plan to live on the Moon. Here's how it would work

children.content: The US space agency has outlined plans for a sprawling lunar outpost involving rovers, hopping drones and resource-extraction technology as part of a renewed race to the Moon.

children.content: The professional misconduct claims were levelled at Australia's first TAFE Electric Vehicle Centre of Excellence.

image.altText: Tesla cars at Hertz.

items.title: Court increases jail sentence for remorseless sex worker killer

children.content: Xiaozheng Lin will spend more time in prison for killing two sex workers in 2022, following an appeal by the prosecutor.

image.altText: Xiaozheng Lin stands against a grey backdrop in a police mug shot.

items.title: Original track designer steers clear of estimated $100m MotoGP remodel

children.content: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

image.altText: A narrow bitumen road runs between trees and large patches of grass

items.title: Maugean skates released after more than two years in captivity

children.content: A pair of endangered rays taken from a Tasmanian harbour as a species "insurance policy" have been released back into the wild after helping to produce more than 400 eggs for a captive research program.

image.altText: A man holds a Maugean skate in the water

items.title: Heavy rain on Gus Lamont's family station prompts police to renew search

children.content: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

items.title: Pentagon denies reports of clash with SpaceX over Starlink price hike

children.content: The Pentagon has denied claims that the department has been clashing with Elon Musk's SpaceX over costs to use its satellite internet network, which the military's kamikaze drones rely upon in the war against Iran.

image.altText: SpaceX founder Elon Musk speaks at a press conference

items.title: Australian students record worst tech literacy results on record

children.content: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

image.altText: A pair of unidentifiable hands type on a laptop, with other people on laptops in the background.

items.title: Copi Mineral Sands Project near Victoria-NSW border gets green light

children.content: A mineral sands project of global strategic importance receives provisional approval from the New South Wales government.

image.altText: Farmland in the Australian outback, with a blue sky, red dirt and dried grass and scrub

items.title: Scott's sacking gives Essendon another chance to step out of Hird shadow

children.content: Essendon finds itself at a clear demarcation point after sacking Brad Scott as the shadow of James Hird once again looms large over the troubled club.

image.altText: James Hird scratches his chin with his right hand

items.title: More than a million worshippers brave searing heat for annual Hajj pilgrimage

children.content: Thousands of Muslim worshippers converge on the rocky hills and plains of Saudi Arabia's Mount Arafat as the annual Islamic pilgrimage, the Hajj, reached its peak.
